name: Breakfast Burritos
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups potato, peeled & cooked, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
15 oz can black beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry bacon pieces in a large skillet until crisp; drain, reserving 1 tablespoon of the drippings in the pan.
2. Add potato, onion, and garlic to the pan. Cook, covered, about 10 minutes or until potatoes start to brown, stirring occasionally.
3. Add black beans and chilies; heat through. (If making ahead, omit heating bean mixture.)
4. Meanwhile, in another large skillet, melt butter, lifting and tilting to cover the surface. Pour in eggs and cook until just set, stirring frequently.
5. Cook just until barely set because the eggs will cook more in the burritos.
6. Stir in bacon and the potato mixture.
7. Grease a baking dish. Lay tortillas flat on a work surface. Divide filling evenly among tortillas and roll up; place seam side down in the baking dish.
8. Spoon Green Chili Sauce over one half of all the tortillas.
9. Spoon Red Chili sauce over the other half of all the tortillas.
10. Bake uncovered at 350°F for 20 to 25 minutes or until heated through.
11. Top with cheese, bake 4 to 5 minutes more or until cheese is melted.
To make ahead:
1. This dish can be made the night before up to the point of adding the chili sauces.
2. Cover tortillas with a damp paper towel to prevent them from cracking, then with plastic wrap. Refrigerate.
3. Remove plastic wrap and towels. Spoon sauces on as above.
4. Bake in a 350°F oven for 20 to 35 minutes or until nearly heated through.
5. Uncover; top with cheese and bake 8 to 10 more minutes or until heated through.
